title: AI / ML Engineer
company_focus:
Global advisory firm offering tax, finance, and business
operations consulting across business lifecycle
description:
BPM – where caring and community are in our company DNA. We
are always striving to be our best selves and are compelled
BPM – where caring and community are in our company DNA. We
are always striving to be our best selves and are compelled
to ask the questions that lead to innovation.

As a [Job Title] at BPM UK, you will be part of an exciting
growth journey as we establish and expand our UK presence.
BPM has a strong global advisory focus, and the UK team is
at the forefront of this expansion. Our people-first culture
sets us apart—we combine the best of UK business practices
with the backing and resources of a well-established
international firm.

You will work with ambitious, high-growth clients, including
recognizable UK brands, as they navigate the complexities of
international expansion. At BPM, we take a true advisory
approach, partnering with clients throughout their business
lifecycle to provide strategic insights across tax, finance,
and business operations.

At BPM, you will broaden your skills, challenge yourself,
and make a difference for your colleagues and communities.
Our entrepreneurial spirit drives us to see and do things
differently. Our passion for people makes BPM a place where
everyone feels welcome, valued, and part of something
bigger. Because People Matter.

What You Get:

International Exposure & Collaboration – Work alongside a
global team, integrating with US colleagues and clients.

Flexible Working Arrangements – Hybrid model with regular
Central London team meetups and colleague events.

Challenging & Exciting Client Work – Serve a diverse client
base, from UK-based high-growth companies to North American
businesses expanding internationally.

A Strong, People-First Culture – We prioritize work-life
balance, career development, and flexibility, setting us
apart from traditional firms.

Professional Growth & Development – Support for ACA, AAT,
and other professional qualifications, with structured
training, mentorship, and learning opportunities.

About the role:

We are seeking an AI/ML Engineer to join BPM’s Enterprise
Technology Solutions team. This role is for a builder —
someone who doesn’t just experiment with AI but makes it
work inside the firm at scale. You’ll take AI tools and
models and embed them into BPM’s day-to-day operations,
ensuring they deliver real business value.

In this role, you’ll design and develop AI-powered
applications — from chatbots and copilots to workflow
automations — that streamline processes across Tax,
Assurance, Advisory, and our Corporate Services Team. You’ll
partner with teams to spot opportunities where AI can cut
manual effort, increase accuracy, and speed up delivery,
then turn those opportunities into working solutions.

At BPM, this isn’t about chasing hype — it’s about applying
AI where it matters most and seeing your work make an
immediate impact. You’ll be part of standing up a brand-new
AI-focused development team, not only building solutions but
also shaping the team’s processes, tools, and standards.
This is a chance to learn new technologies, push technical
boundaries, and set the foundation for how AI gets delivered
across the firm. Success in this role means turning ideas
into reliable solutions colleagues can depend on — helping
BPM move faster, work smarter, and stay ahead in a rapidly
changing industry.



Key Responsibilities:



Model Development: Design and evaluate AI/ML models tailored
to BPM use cases (e.g., tax AI bot, Copilot extensions).

Data Engineering: Prepare and manage structured and
unstructured datasets, ensuring quality inputs for training
and evaluation.

AI Integration: Build and deploy AI features into BPM
systems and workflows, using tools like Copilot Studio,
Azure OpenAI, and Power Platform.

Automation: Deliver automation solutions that streamline
operations and reduce manual effort for Tax, Assurance, and
Advisory practices.

Collaboration: Work closely with IT, business stakeholders,
and data governance teams to identify use cases and ensure
solutions align with firm goals.

Experimentation & Innovation: Rapidly prototype AI
solutions, test emerging tools, and recommend best practices
for adoption.

Governance & Quality: Apply responsible AI practices,
ensuring models are accurate, explainable, and compliant
with BPM’s standards.



Required Skills & Experience

- Strong background in machine learning, data science, and
  applied AI.
- Proficiency with Azure OpenAI, Copilot Studio, and Microsoft
  ecosystem tools.
- Experience building chatbots, copilots, or similar AI-driven
  automation.
- Solid programming skills with Python.
- Knowledge of MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a plus.
- Experience preparing datasets and building ML pipelines.
- Familiarity with product development lifecycles and agile
  delivery practices.
- Security-conscious mindset with knowledge of data protection
  best practices, especially when working with sensitive or
  regulated data.
- Strong problem-solving skills with ability to translate
  business needs into AI solutions.
- Excellent collaboration and communication skills to work
  across technical and non-technical teams.
- Ability to explain complex AI concepts simply to
  stakeholders.
- Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field along with at least 5
  years of related work experience

Preferred Qualifications

- Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred)
- Familiarity with frameworks and libraries such as OpenAI
  SDK, Azure AI SDK, LangChain, and Pandas.
- Knowledge of enterprise data governance, compliance, and
  responsible AI practices.
- Prior experience in professional services (accounting,
  consulting, or similar) a plus.

Knowledge & Experience

- At least 1 year of applied AI experience
- Proficiency with Azure OpenAI, Copilot Studio, Power
  Platform, and Microsoft ecosystem tools, with proven
  success embedding AI solutions into enterprise
  environments.
- Strong programming background in Python and SQL, with
  experience using frameworks and libraries like LangChain,
  Pandas, and Azure AI SDK.
- Experience building and deploying chatbots, Copilot
  extensions, and automation workflows that reduce manual
  work and improve efficiency.
- Skilled in data engineering and pipeline development,
  including preparation of structured and unstructured
  datasets for AI/ML use cases.
- Solid understanding of cloud platforms (Azure preferred),
  enterprise data governance, and responsible AI practices.
- Strong collaboration skills with ability to translate
  technical concepts into plain language for non-technical
  stakeholders.
- Able to work independently or in collaborative, cross-
  functional teams to deliver AI and data-driven solutions
  and helping shape a newly implemented Ai focused team at
  BPM.
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with ability to
  adapt quickly to shifting priorities and emerging
  technologies.
- Able to adjust quickly to the fast-evolving AI/ML landscape
- Skilled at translating complex technical concepts into plain
  language for non-technical colleagues and leadership.
- Effective at building trust and collaboration across diverse
  teams, including technical staff, business stakeholders,
  and leadership.
- Naturally curious, persuasive, and motivated to drive
  adoption of innovative technologies.
- Strong written and verbal communicator, with experience
  preparing clear documentation, training materials, and
  presentations.
- Adept at researching new AI tools, frameworks, and industry
  trends, and applying them to practical business use cases.
- Customer- and colleague-focused mindset, ensuring AI
  solutions align with real-world needs.
- Highly organized with ability to prioritize and execute
  tasks in high-pressure, deadline-driven environments.
- Persistent and results-oriented, with a track record of
  seeing projects through to completion.

**************

BPM provides equal employment opportunities to all employees
and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination
and harassment of any type without regard to race, color,
religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status,
genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation,
gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic
protected by federal, state or local laws.

For positions based in San Francisco, consideration of
qualified candidates with arrest and conviction records will
be in a manner consistent with the San Francisco Fair Chance
Ordinance.

Please note - this posting is for prospective candidates
only. Unsolicited third-party resume submissions will be
considered property of BPM and will not be acknowledged or
returned.

About BPM UK:



BPM is an established global advisory firm, and our UK
expansion is a key part of our growth journey. We specialise
in partnering with clients throughout their business
lifecycle, supporting everything from domestic tax and audit
compliance to international expansion strategies. Unlike
traditional firms, BPM offers a true global advisory
experience, ensuring clients receive proactive, strategic
guidance.



Our UK team brings together the best of UK business
practices with BPM’s international expertise, fostering a
collaborative, flexible, and people-first culture.



We are looking for driven professionals who are excited to
be part of something new—helping shape BPM’s future in the
UK while working with some of the most dynamic and
recognisable brands in the market.

use_cases: Tax automation through AI-powered chatbots and copilots that handle tax-related queries and workflows
Document processing and analysis for Assurance and Advisory teams using NLP and ML models
Workflow automation across corporate services to reduce manual effort and increase operational efficiency

Enterprise AI applications that provide strategic insights and support decision-making across business operations
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is an AI/ML Engineer role that involves hands-on
building and deploying AI solutions - designing chatbots,
copilots, developing AI/ML models, and embedding AI tools
into enterprise workflows. The role explicitly states it's
for "a builder" who makes AI work at scale, including
working with Azure OpenAI, Copilot Studio, and LangChain to
create functional AI applications.
